title: Whiskey Business

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Episode opening with Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.punchline: The iconic catchphrase itself
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - perfectly economical and character-specific but not original anymore

jokes.text: You're deleting the 2009 Oscar Red Carpet! There were some awesome dresses! And quite a few... disasters!
jokes.setup: Homer defending his TV recordings when Marge wants to delete them
jokes.punchline: Homer's unexpected investment in Oscar fashion commentary
jokes.explanation: Solid character comedy showing Homer's surprising interests and vanity about fashion

jokes.text: I can't part with a single one of my Hoarders, hands off my episodes of Episodes
jokes.setup: Homer listing TV shows he won't delete
jokes.punchline: The wordplay of 'episodes of Episodes' and the irony of hoarding Hoarders
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with meta-commentary on TV watching habits - well-crafted double joke

jokes.text: For every one of my cartoons that you erase, I rip up a family vacation photo. And just so you know I mean business, here's an ear!
jokes.setup: Homer threatening retaliation for deleted shows
jokes.punchline: Immediately following through with extreme action - ripping up photos
jokes.explanation: Good escalation comedy with Homer's characteristic extreme reactions and childish negotiation tactics

jokes.text: It's not what you think! So I stayed up for the last 87 hours watching all my show
jokes.setup: Homer defending keeping 'American Masters: Kitty Carlisle'
jokes.punchline: The absurd 87-hour viewing marathon and defensive 'it's not what you think'
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist humor with Homer's excessive TV watching habits

jokes.text: I'm asking if a priest can beat a big cat in a death match in some kind of polygon. Hexa or octa? Only one way to decide: arm wrestling.
jokes.setup: Moe trying to get attention by asking about Padres vs Tigers
jokes.punchline: Complete misunderstanding turning it into an absurd combat scenario
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist comedy with Moe's characteristic misunderstanding and escalation to violence

jokes.text: Eh, who am I kidding? They don't care about me.
jokes.setup: Moe's elaborate death match scenario gets no response
jokes.punchline: Immediate deflation and self-awareness about being ignored
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moe's desperation and self-awareness

jokes.text: I'm begging you, please, please show me some love! Yeah, it's nothing like that.
jokes.setup: Moe claiming he's not crying for help
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself by literally begging for love
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic contradiction comedy - Moe's desperation overrides his attempted dignity

jokes.text: You said, 'Business problems.' Is that correct? No! I got nothing and no one! You said, 'Face sucked off by vacuum cleaner.' Is that correct?
jokes.setup: Moe trying to use suicide hotline automated system
jokes.punchline: System completely misunderstanding and offering increasingly bizarre options
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of bureaucratic absurdity with increasingly specific bizarre options

jokes.text: Hello, you have reached the Buzz Cola Suicide Hotline. Our options have changed, so please listen carefully.
jokes.setup: Moe calls suicide hotline for help
jokes.punchline: Corporate automated system for suicide prevention
jokes.explanation: Dark but brilliant satire of corporate phone systems applied to suicide prevention - very Simpsons

jokes.text: ♪ Suicide is painless ♪ It brings on many changes ♪
jokes.setup: Moe on hold with suicide hotline
jokes.punchline: Playing the M*A*S*H theme song about suicide as hold music
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy reference using inappropriate music for suicide hotline hold - very Simpsons

jokes.text: Hi, I'm looking for a Mr. Ron, first name Moe. Moe Ron? Moron.
jokes.setup: Bart calling with a prank name
jokes.punchline: Classic Moe Ron = Moron prank call
jokes.explanation: Classic callback to the show's running gag - well-executed but not original

jokes.text: I took a class where you do chest compressions to a Bee Gees song. ♪ How deep is your love? ♪
jokes.setup: Homer knows CPR and mentions doing it to a Bee Gees song
jokes.punchline: Using wrong Bee Gees song instead of 'Staying Alive'
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection comedy playing on common CPR training knowledge

jokes.text: Are you sure it wasn't 'Staying Alive?' Too on the nose.
jokes.setup: Barney correcting Homer about CPR song
jokes.punchline: Homer dismissing the obvious choice as 'too on the nose'
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about avoiding obvious jokes while making the obvious joke

jokes.text: Who's the president now? Some jerk. He's back!
jokes.setup: Testing if Moe is mentally okay after revival
jokes.punchline: Moe's cynical but accurate assessment proves he's fine
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about political cynicism as a sign of mental health

jokes.text: Ah, this post-suicide afterglow gets shorter every time.
jokes.setup: Moe briefly feeling better after being saved
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to this being a recurring pattern
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy treating suicide attempts casually - very character-specific for Moe

jokes.text: We could write on his face when he passes out. Yeah, that's always good for a laugh.
jokes.setup: Trying to think of ways to help Moe
jokes.punchline: Suggesting cruel pranks instead of actual help
jokes.explanation: Standard cruel humor showing the characters' lack of empathy

jokes.text: Four guys, a chick and a noose. Just like the movies I like to watch.
jokes.setup: Moe commenting on the group going on a trip
jokes.punchline: Revealing his disturbing taste in movies
jokes.explanation: Good dark character comedy revealing Moe's disturbing interests

jokes.text: Wow, look at all them countdown clocks. Acres of rainforest left, deaths by lung cancer. It's magical.
jokes.setup: Moe commenting on Capital City
jokes.punchline: Finding depressing statistics 'magical'
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor showing Moe's pessimistic worldview

jokes.text: Capital City has a twin? Moved to California to be a star, but it just turned into Glendale.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining about Capital City's twin
jokes.punchline: The anticlimatic reality of becoming Glendale instead of Hollywood
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about failed dreams and suburban disappointment

jokes.text: Gentlemen's Whorehouse? Whoa, ho, ho! No, no, it's a Wearhouse.
jokes.setup: Moe misreading the clothing store sign
jokes.punchline: The obvious mishearing and correction
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection joke with predictable setup and payoff

jokes.text: They're like a wedding ring; I can't take them off anymore.
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on Homer's blue pants
jokes.punchline: Comparing clothing to marriage commitment due to Homer's weight gain
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about long marriages and physical changes

jokes.text: Nah, it's a 44 long. I wear a 38 hunched.
jokes.setup: Moe trying on a suit
jokes.punchline: His specific size accounting for his hunched posture
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor that perfectly captures Moe's physical appearance and self-awareness

jokes.text: Tent City is across the street. Tent, eh?
jokes.setup: Salesman dismissively directing Moe to Tent City
jokes.punchline: Moe actually considering wearing a tent as clothing
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of clothing desperation with visual comedy potential

jokes.text: No tents, no barrels, no kiddie pools. Thank you, fashion police!
jokes.setup: Marge preventing Moe from wearing inappropriate clothing
jokes.punchline: The increasingly absurd list of what Moe might consider wearing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how desperate Moe's fashion situation is

jokes.text: You mean my sister? She's your sister? The plot thickens. Clueless little Lisa doesn't suspect a thing.
jokes.setup: Bart and Martin planning something secretive
jokes.punchline: Acting like Lisa being Bart's sister is a shocking plot twist
jokes.explanation: Decent ironic humor about treating obvious family relationships as conspiracy plot twists

jokes.text: Jellyroll Jones, Boston Cream Basie, Birthmark Billy Jackson, Grabass Walker, the Fatneck Sisters
jokes.setup: Lisa listing jazz musicians at a hologram performance
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd parody names of famous jazz musicians
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay escalating from clever to crude jazz name parodies

jokes.text: Lights and magic, Lisa. You don't need anything else. But I took a bunch of stuff anyway.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining he only needs lights and magic for the hologram
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself about taking extra equipment
jokes.explanation: Standard contradiction humor showing Bart's lack of confidence in his simple plan

jokes.text: I gave him a caramel. He should have been chewing for hours. I couldn't unwrap it!
jokes.setup: Bart explaining why Grampa should be distracted
jokes.punchline: Grampa's inability to unwrap candy due to age
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy about aging and Grampa's limitations

jokes.text: I don't want to die! I want to still be a burden!
jokes.setup: Grampa falling and fearing death
jokes.punchline: His aspiration to continue being a burden rather than just survive
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy with perfect self-awareness about his family role - highly quotable

jokes.text: Man, I must real love him.
jokes.setup: Bart forced to kiss Grampa's forehead
jokes.punchline: Bart's surprised realization about his own feelings
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's emotional growth through reluctant action

jokes.text: If the Wilsons' calf is birthed, Doc Miller should be available.
jokes.setup: Discussion of getting medical help for Grampa
jokes.punchline: Reference to old-timey rural medical priorities
jokes.explanation: Good period-appropriate humor showing Grampa's outdated worldview

jokes.text: My tailbone! And it was my last good bone.
jokes.setup: Grampa trying to leave but falling down
jokes.punchline: Claiming his tailbone was his last functional body part
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of age-related physical deterioration humor

jokes.text: 'Sorry, pal'? Hey, that's common courtesy. The kind I've only seen in movies. This suit is amazing!
jokes.setup: Someone apologizes to Moe politely
jokes.punchline: Moe being amazed by basic human decency due to his suit
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing how little kindness Moe normally receives

jokes.text: Dirt. Carpet. Another layer of dirt. Congoleum. Hardwood! The perfect floor for doing a happy jig.
jokes.setup: Moe cleaning his bar and discovering layers underneath
jokes.punchline: Archaeological excavation revealing surprisingly good flooring and his joy about dancing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with visual comedy potential and character transformation

jokes.text: Wow, non-losers? Huh, I never thought I'd see the day.
jokes.setup: Successful-looking customers entering Moe's bar
jokes.punchline: Moe's shock at attracting normal clientele
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware character comedy about Moe's usual customer base

jokes.text: It'll either be the best thing you ever had or the last thing you'll ever have.
jokes.setup: Moe offering his homemade bourbon
jokes.punchline: Honest advertising about potentially lethal alcohol
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about Moe's questionable brewing skills with honest marketing

jokes.text: We turn dreams into money that mostly goes to us, but you get a little.
jokes.setup: Venture capitalists explaining their business
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest description of exploitative business practices
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical humor about venture capitalism with perfect economy

jokes.text: If your feet touch the ground, we've failed.
jokes.setup: Promising luxury treatment for Moe
jokes.punchline: Absurdly literal interpretation of being elevated
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist humor about luxury service promises

jokes.text: I've made a lot of mistakes, but liking this ain't one of them.
jokes.setup: Focus group testing Moe's bourbon
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment acknowledging personal failures
jokes.explanation: Decent self-deprecating humor typical of Springfield residents

jokes.text: Come on, blue! Come on, pink! After a pulse-pounding race, hypertension pills win again. Call me crazy, but I can't stop betting on dementia.
jokes.setup: Bart making Grampa's medication time into a game
jokes.punchline: Treating serious medical conditions as gambling entertainment
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy turning elderly care into sport - very creative premise

jokes.text: Call me crazy, but I can't stop betting on dementia.
jokes.setup: Continuing the medication gambling theme
jokes.punchline: Betting on Grampa's mental decline
jokes.explanation: Dark but economical - continues the gambling metaphor into uncomfortable territory

jokes.text: Hot, oh! Cold, oh! Thank God I'm not in the shower!
jokes.setup: Bart turning hot water on and off while Homer showers
jokes.punchline: Homer's relief about not being the victim of the prank he's experiencing
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy with Homer's characteristic obliviousness

jokes.text: My crotch is crotchety, sitting down makes me uppity, and you call these cups pleated?!
jokes.setup: Grampa listing his complaints to Bart
jokes.punchline: Wordplay mixing physical complaints with personality traits
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining physical ailments with personality descriptors - quintessential Grampa

jokes.text: From Tupac Shakur to Dwight Eisenhower, holograms have introduced some of our leading dead people to a new audience of people with money.
jokes.setup: Defending hologram technology
jokes.punchline: Bizarre pairing of Tupac and Eisenhower as comparable entertainers
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor with unexpected historical figure pairing - very quotable

jokes.text: ♪ My name is Princess Di ♪ And I'm here to say ♪ When I watch a DVD, it's got to be Blu-ray. ♪
jokes.setup: Hologram advertising getting more inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Dead Princess Diana rapping about technology in poor taste
jokes.explanation: Bold dark comedy using inappropriate celebrity exploitation - risks being offensive but commits to the bit

jokes.text: That is the most accurate picture of my face made of girl-parts that I have ever seen.
jokes.setup: Models creating body art portrait of Moe
jokes.punchline: Moe's oddly specific appreciation suggesting this has happened before
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist character comedy with Moe's characteristic inappropriate specificity

jokes.text: Enjoy your last night as a Democrat.
jokes.setup: Venture capitalists leaving Moe for the night
jokes.punchline: Implying wealth automatically changes political affiliation
jokes.explanation: Sharp political humor with good economy - typical Simpsons social commentary

jokes.text: This building's got 98 floors! And I'm going down to P-6! P-6!
jokes.setup: Moe's suit caught in elevator door
jokes.punchline: Extreme descent from 98th floor to parking level P-6
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation comedy with physical consequences - serviceable but not exceptional

jokes.text: I didn't go to movies as a kid. Spent all my time at the pierogi factory. Dab the potato, fold the dough. That was my Star Wars.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining why he doesn't know Dumbo
jokes.punchline: Comparing repetitive factory work to epic entertainment
jokes.explanation: Excellent character backstory with perfect metaphor - 'That was my Star Wars' is beautifully melancholy

jokes.text: The crows weren't racist, the people who drew them were.
jokes.setup: Homer calling Dumbo crows racist
jokes.punchline: Marge making sophisticated distinction about representation vs. creators
jokes.explanation: Sharp meta-commentary on Disney's problematic history delivered with Marge's characteristic insight

jokes.text: I've been faking being hurt, just like I fake liking those terrible homemade Christmas gifts you give. Did she? No. Even she thought it was crap.
jokes.setup: Grampa admitting to faking injury
jokes.punchline: Escalating to brutal honesty about Bart's gifts and Marge's politeness
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy with Grampa's characteristic brutal honesty escalating to family truths

jokes.text: Well, you are the first thing I took care of that didn't die. Oh, I hear that a lot.
jokes.setup: Bart reflecting on caring for Grampa
jokes.punchline: Grampa's casual acknowledgment of being frequently told this dark fact
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with perfect deadpan delivery - very quotable and character-specific

jokes.text: It's Quasimodo without the pathos!
jokes.setup: Moe arriving disheveled at stock exchange
jokes.punchline: Literary reference to hunchback but noting absence of sympathetic qualities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literary insult with perfect economy - sophisticated humor with mean-spirited edge

jokes.text: At least I don't have to train the new bartender to make what I like-- beer to the top.
jokes.setup: Barney commenting on Moe being back
jokes.punchline: Revealing his drink order is just 'fill the glass completely'
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Barney's simple but specific drinking preferences

tmdb_overview: Moe is flying high after venture capitalists take a shine to his homemade whiskey, while Grampa, after injuring himself babysitting Bart, decides he'd rather be looked after by his grandson than be at the nursing home. Also, Lisa voices her displeasure at a hologram of music legend Bleeding Gums Murphy, and Sonny Rollins (as himself) shows up to ask her to stop all that jazz about boycotting a record label.
